disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Preceding viral upper respiratory tract symptoms (clear rhinorrhoea, dry cough) for three days before the acute ear pain
trigger_question_examples: Has he been unwell in any other way recently, like a cold?
Did anything come before the ear pain — runny nose, cough?
How has he been over the last few days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, he's had a runny nose and a bit of a dry cough for about three days — just a normal cold I thought. The ear thing only started tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute severe unilateral (left) otalgia with ear-tugging, of sudden onset, worse over 12 hours
trigger_question_examples: Which ear is bothering him?
Can you tell me more about the ear pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's his left ear. He keeps grabbing at it and screaming — it came on really suddenly a few hours ago and it's been getting worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New reduced hearing / not orienting to soft sounds on the affected (left) side — conductive hearing loss
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem to be hearing okay on that side?
Have you noticed him not responding to sounds on the sore ear side?
Any change in his hearing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask, he doesn't seem to turn to me when I talk quietly on his left side. I didn't think much of it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No neck stiffness, no excessive drowsiness or difficulty rousing, no non-blanching rash, no persistent vomiting — meningism screen negative
trigger_question_examples: Is his neck stiff, or is he unusually sleepy or hard to wake?
Any rash that doesn't fade when you press on it, or repeated vomiting?
Is he floppy or difficult to rouse at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — his neck moves fine, he's not that sleepy, no rash, and he hasn't been vomiting. He's upset but he's with it.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: He just seems really sore, I haven't checked for anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No redness, swelling or tenderness of the bone behind the ear and the ear is not pushed forward/outward — mastoiditis screen negative
trigger_question_examples: Is there any redness or swelling on the bone just behind his ear?
Does the ear look like it's being pushed forwards, or is the area behind it swollen or tender?
Can you look behind the sore ear for me — any lump, redness or swelling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I just had a look — behind his ear looks normal, no swelling or redness, and the ear isn't sticking out or anything.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really looked behind his ear.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No discharge of fluid, pus or blood from the ear canal — tympanic membrane perforation absent at presentation
trigger_question_examples: Is there any fluid, pus or blood coming out of his ear?
Have you seen any discharge from the ear canal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing's come out of his ear — it's dry, just the pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child remains alert, consolable, feeding/drinking with wet nappies — no features of dehydration or systemic sepsis
trigger_question_examples: Is he still drinking and having wet nappies?
Can you settle him at all, and is he otherwise his usual self between the crying?
How is he in himself apart from the ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's still taking his drink bottle and had a wet nappy not long ago. When I cuddle him he settles for a bit, then the pain sets him off again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Only a single sub-therapeutic dose of paracetamol given ~4 hours ago; no ibuprofen given; no regular analgesia schedule
trigger_question_examples: What have you given him for the pain, and how much?
When did he last have any pain relief?
Has he had any ibuprofen or just the Panadol?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I gave him a small dose of children's Panadol about four hours ago but I wasn't sure how much to give, so it was probably not enough. He hasn't had any Nurofen.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pain on moving/pulling the outer ear (pinna/tragus) and no itch or debris in the ear canal — argues against otitis externa
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt more if you gently wiggle or tug the outer part of his ear?
Is the ear itchy, or is there any gunk or flaking in the ear opening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It doesn't seem worse when I touch the outside of his ear, and I can't see any gunk or flakiness in it — it looks clear.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Does he need antibiotics? My friend's little one got antibiotics straight away for an ear infection.
Should I be taking him to the emergency department tonight?
